Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Californias Anti-Slapp Statute, Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(C)(1)
Off Calendar Mar-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TERRENCE HOWARD's Motion For Attorneys Fees is granted in part. Mr. Howard is awarded attorney's fees against plaintiff Ron Newt in the amount of $24,000. No opposition filed. Mr. Howard is entitled to an award of fees since he succeeded in having all claims alleged against him dismissed on his anti-SLAPP motion. Mr. Howard's request for a fee award of $43,521.50 is excessive given the issues involved in this motion, the anti-SLAPP motion, and the fact that Mr. Howard and others had previously filed a similar anti-SLAPP motion and motion for fees in case 554437 which largely applied to this case. While the court does not question that counsel for Mr. Howard spent the hours that they say they spent and Mr. Howard is permitted to have them spend those hours, the hours spent far exceed what was reasonably necessary to succeed on the motions. The court concludes that one week of work (40 hours) at an hourly rate of $600 is a reasonable amount of hours to spend on these motions.

ORDER SIGNED after hearing.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
